route.name: Shasta Siskiyou Loop
route.description: Official/public GPX route with forest roads, trail pieces, chunky/washy sections, and Mount Shasta-area connectors.
route.surfaceMix: Official/public GPX route with forest roads, trail pieces, chunky/washy sections, and Mount Shasta-area connectors.
route.bestSeason: Mostly June to October; refresh Shasta-Trinity closures, fire restrictions, heat, and smoke.

difficultyProfile.surface: Official/public GPX route with forest roads, trail pieces, chunky/washy sections, and Mount Shasta-area connectors.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Mount Shasta/Lake Siskiyou services plus limited north-side sources; verify exact gaps.
resources.food: Mount Shasta and Weed before/after; carry route food.
resources.camping: Lake Siskiyou, NF campgrounds, or legal dispersed options must be confirmed for July 10-13.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before gate confirmation.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Do not promote until Gateway/FR31 closure overlap is checked against the GPX.
Use wider tires than pure gravel.
Fri-Sun or Sat-Mon is more realistic than a fast Sat-Sun push.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control gate confirmation.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: promote to plan_ready source coverage only; gate status remains blocked until date-specific gates pass
blockers: Gateway Trails closure and Forest Road 31/bypass status may affect the route.
Shasta-Siskiyou Loop cannot be gate-confirmed until active Shasta-Trinity fire restrictions, closure overlap, camps, and water are checked against the GPX.
Distance fits the weekend, but route cannot be gate-confirmed until FR31/bypass legality, Gateway/USFS trail status, exact camps, and water gaps are verified against the GPX.
Active Shasta-Trinity fire restrictions constrain camp/cooking choices.
North-side water is explicitly limited and not independently confirmed for July 10-13.
requiredFixes: Check imported GPX against Gateway Trails closure and FR31/bypass.
Refresh north-side water/food, fire, smoke, and camp plan.
Overlay the imported GPX against Shasta-Trinity and Klamath current closure/forest-order maps.
Select exact July camps/lodging and confirm they are open and legal under current fire restrictions.
Measure water/resupply gaps from the GPX and confirm current source reliability.
Refresh fire restrictions, smoke, forecast, and road/trail status within 24-48 hours of departure.
Overlay imported GPX against Shasta-Trinity and Klamath current closure/forest-order maps.
Overlay imported GPX and any FR31 bypass against current Shasta-Trinity and Klamath alerts/orders/maps.
Confirm exact night 1 and night 2 legal camps or lodging for July 10-13.
Confirm Headwaters Spring, Pilgrim Creek, Ash Creek, Weed, and Lake Siskiyou water/resupply status.
For Gate Confirmed, overlay the GPX against official access and closure sources.
For Gate Confirmed, choose exact overnight, water/resupply, bailout, and parking plan.
For Gate Confirmed, refresh weather, smoke, fire, and road/park alerts close to departure.
openQuestions: What changed in the July 9-10 final refresh for closures, weather, smoke, water, and camps?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: The route may be segmentable for next weekend, but the full Shasta-Siskiyou itinerary needs exact duration and camp sequence confirmation.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Official/public GPX has been imported and parsed locally.
promotionChecklist.campingPermitsNote: Specific July camps/lodging are not selected or confirmed and must comply with Shasta-Trinity fire restrictions.
promotionChecklist.closuresFireWeatherNote: Shasta-Trinity 2026 Fire Restrictions are active July 1 through December 31, 2026; Klamath also lists Very High fire danger. The GPX needs current closure/fire/smoke review before use.
promotionChecklist.legalAccessNote: The GPX must be checked against Shasta-Trinity and Klamath current alerts, forest orders, and any road/trail closures before promotion.
promotionChecklist.waterResupplyNote: Water/resupply gaps are not measured from the GPX and current sources are not verified.

days.name: Shasta Siskiyou Loop segment 1
days.camp: Lake Siskiyou, NF campgrounds, or legal dispersed options must be confirmed for July 10-13.

waypoints.name: Delta should be crossable here in the summer season after most snow in the mountains is gone. June-Oct
Many roads to bypass tracks along the way to Hwy 97
Trails west of here to forest land and primitive camping.
River Crossing Option over logs when water is too high, use caution
Might be possible to get to lake this way, but not scouted yet.

logistics: Final gate confirmation requires live date-specific resource and legal checks.
Shasta-Trinity National Forest fire restriction source checked July 2, 2026: https://www.fs.usda.gov/r05/shasta-trinity/alerts/2026-fire-restrictions-forest-order
Klamath National Forest source checked July 2, 2026: https://www.fs.usda.gov/r05/klamath

readinessEvidence.finding: Shasta-Trinity National Forest 2026 Fire Restrictions are effective July 1 through December 31, 2026 and restrict fires/campfires/stove fires except in listed developed recreation or designated fire-safe sites; gas/pressurized-fuel stove use requires a valid California Campfire Permit and clearance.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Shasta-Trinity National Forest 2026 Fire Restrictions Forest Order

readinessEvidence.finding: Shasta-Trinity lists active alerts including the 2026 Fire Restrictions Forest Order, plus Moderate fire danger for Mount Shasta and McCloud zones.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Shasta-Trinity National Forest 2026 Fire Restrictions Forest Order

readinessEvidence.finding: Klamath National Forest lists Very High fire danger for both the East Zone - Goosenest Ranger District and West Zone, relevant to northern California/Southern Oregon route corridors.
No confirmed July camp sequence, route-corridor closure audit, or water gap measurement is recorded for this GPX-backed route.
Shasta-Trinity lists 2026 Fire Restrictions starting July 1, 2026 plus a Gateway Area Trails Closure Order affecting Strawberry Valley, Magma, and Deadwood trails.
Klamath lists Very High fire danger for relevant zones and links to fire/smoke resources.
Public GPX route is 84 miles / 2-3 days, but route source notes FR31 closures/bypass and very limited north-side food/water.
Fire restrictions effective July 1-December 31, 2026; fires/campfires/stove fires restricted except listed developed/designated sites, gas stove use requires valid California Campfire Permit.
Usable route geometry source is identified for planning and local map/export QA.
Official land-manager or route-corridor source identified for route-mile legal access overlay.
